_comment: Citation registry. Each species and management guide references these by id. Add your own sources here (e.g. your CIG trial reports) and reference them from local-observations.json.

nrcs-appalachia.title: Best Management of Pasture and Hayland Species in Appalachia
nrcs-appalachia.org: USDA NRCS Appalachian Plant Materials Center
nrcs-appalachia.note: Regional species adaptation and management guidance for the Appalachian region.

wvu-species.title: Forage Species
wvu-species.note: Species-by-species adaptation notes for West Virginia.

wvu-production.title: Pasture and Hay Production Potential in West Virginia
wvu-production.note: Yield and adaptation context for WV forages.

wvu-grazing.title: Grazing for Appalachian Sustainability
wvu-grazing.note: Rotational grazing and pasture management for Appalachian producers.

wvu-fertility.title: Soil Fertility & Fertilizers
wvu-fertility.note: Soil testing, liming, and fertility management.

vt-steep-pastures.title: Managing Virginia's Steep Pastures (VCE 418-005)
vt-steep-pastures.note: Species and grazing management for sloping Appalachian pastures.

psu-establishment.title: Successful Forage Crop Establishment
psu-establishment.note: Seeding depth, timing, seedbed, and no-till/frost seeding guidance.

psu-renovation.title: Hay and Pasture Renovation Decisions
psu-renovation.note: When and how to renovate versus reseed pastures.

psu-tall-fescue.title: Tall Fescue
psu-tall-fescue.note: Adaptation, endophyte, and management of tall fescue.

osu-fescue-ph.title: Tall Fescue Monograph: pH Suitability
osu-fescue-ph.org: Oregon State University Forage Information System
osu-fescue-ph.note: Detailed pH tolerance data for tall fescue.

msu-seeding-rates.title: Forage Seeding Rates When Seeded Alone or in Mixtures
msu-seeding-rates.note: Seeding-rate reference for monocultures and mixes.

mu-birdsfoot.title: Birdsfoot Trefoil (G4640)
mu-birdsfoot.note: Establishment and management of birdsfoot trefoil.

nrcs-plants.title: PLANTS Database & Plant Guides
nrcs-plants.note: Species fact sheets and plant guides, including native warm-season grasses.

forage-upick.title: Forage U-Pick
forage-upick.note: Precedent tool cited in the CIG grant that inspired this planner.

ssurgo.title: Web Soil Survey / SSURGO (Soil Data Access)
ssurgo.note: Authoritative soil map-unit data (pH, drainage class, slope, available water) queried by location for the address lookup.

usgs-3dep.title: 3DEP Elevation Point Query Service
usgs-3dep.note: Elevation, slope, and aspect for the address lookup.

open-meteo.title: Historical Weather / Climate API (ERA5, 1991–2020 normals)
open-meteo.note: Downscaled climate normals used to derive hardiness zone, frost-free days, and annual rainfall for the address lookup.

nominatim.title: Nominatim Geocoding
nominatim.note: Converts a typed address to coordinates in the browser. The address is used once and never stored.

climatena.title: ClimateNA
climatena.org: Wang, Hamann, Spittlehouse & Carroll (Univ. of British Columbia)
climatena.note: Downscaled climate reference named in the CIG grant. Desktop/batch tool with no browser API; Open-Meteo normals are used as the live substitute. ClimateNA point exports can be added later.
